title: Free Botox
description:
‘I sensed, if you can believe it, that I was being fobbed off.’

Amber Husain on refusing free Botox.

The post Free Botox appeared first on Granta.
hook: She turned down free Botox and wrote about the refusal itself—a small rebellion that reveals everything about aging, beauty, and who gets to decide.

title: Beautiful Sentences Matter: Billy-Ray Belcourt and Matt Hooley
description:
“Queer theory in general spoke to me in an embodied way, beyond its important theoretical work, because it revealed for me that beautiful sentences matter and that they can be critical aspects of the reading experience.”

The post Beautiful Sentences Matter: Billy-Ray Belcourt and Matt Hooley appeared first on Public Books.
hook: A queer theorist on why beautiful sentences aren't decoration but activism—how style and politics collapse into one another in the work that matters.

title: They Will Kill You Took the Blood, Sweat and Tears of Kirill Sokolov, Zazie Beetz, and Myha’la
description: It’s the closing night of the 2026 SXSW Film Festival and They Will Kill You director Kirill Sokolov is taking to the stage of the Paramount Theat
hook: Inside the making of a gory action film that demolishes the family ideal—how a first-time English-language director wrung blood and meaning from genre.

title: In Town, Earlier Than Usual
description: The women wearing this style were carrying cups of coffee. They were hurrying and looked serious and hard-pressed, as though they were quite high-powered – account managers for companies transitioning from advertising or graphic design to AI-collaborative advertising or AI-collaborative graphic design perhaps – and were struggling to accept the effort they had to put in to merely stand still in a role that wasn’t nearly as much fun as they had once imagined it might be. I didn’t know whether to
hook: What the women wearing a certain style right now tell us about labor, AI, and the look of being perpetually overworked in 2026.

title: She never dances alone
description:
Broadcast at Times Square, this kaleidoscopic reimagining of a powwow dance celebrates the strength of Indigenous women

- by Aeon Video
hook: An Indigenous woman's dance exists only in broadcast footage—a meditation on presence, community, and what we lose when performance becomes recording.

title: How Photography Helped Build the Atomic Bomb
description: A group of intergenerational artists, collectively known as Slow War Against the Nuclear State, investigates the afterlife of nuclear politics in a haunting, timely exhibition
hook: How photography became the secret infrastructure of the atomic bomb—a haunting investigation of images, evidence, and nuclear afterlife.

title: Benjamin Wood: ‘John Fowles’s The Magus was so frustrating I threw it at the wall’
description:
The author on the Steinbeck novel that moved him to tears, how becoming a father inspired him to reread Marilynne Robinson, and the culinary comforts of James M Cain

My earliest reading memory
When I was eight, my mother bought me Stanley Bagshaw and the Short-sighted Football Trainer by Bob Wilson. I grew up thinking he was the same Bob Wilson who played in goal for Arsenal and presented sport on ITV. That wasn’t true, but it has never dampened my appr
hook: A novelist on the books that wrecked him and remade him—from the one he threw at the wall to the ones he re-reads as a father.
